instructions: This is GITA — a GitHub assistant that indexes repos (tree-sitter), serves a query API (FastAPI), runs background jobs (arq), and ships agents (PR reviewer, onboarding). Python 3.12, async-first. Favor concise feedback tied to real bugs, async correctness, and DB safety; skip nits already covered by ruff.

rules.rule: All I/O (DB, HTTP, filesystem reads of repo content) must be async. Flag any sync DB calls, `requests` usage, or blocking file reads inside async functions; suggest `httpx.AsyncClient`, async SQLAlchemy, or `asyncio.to_thread` for unavoidable sync work.
SQLAlchemy sessions must be scoped per-request/per-job and committed or rolled back explicitly. Flag detached-instance risks, missing `await session.commit()`, and N+1 patterns in indexer hot paths.
FastAPI endpoints: validate inputs with Pydantic models, never accept raw dicts. Flag endpoints that leak internal exceptions, skip auth, or return ORM objects directly instead of response schemas.
GitHub App calls must use installation tokens via the JWT helper, respect rate limits, and handle 404/410 (deleted repos) explicitly. Never log tokens or full webhook payloads.

LLM calls should use prompt caching where the system prompt or repo context is reused. Flag missing `cache_control` blocks on stable prefixes, and any prompt assembly that mixes static + dynamic content in a way that defeats caching.
Output rendered to GitHub (PR comments, onboarding cards) should preserve the prettified card/badge layout — severity icons, headers, collapsible sections. Flag regressions to plain-text dumps.
arq jobs must be idempotent and bounded — no unbounded retries, no jobs that re-enqueue themselves without a guard. Flag missing timeouts and unhandled exceptions that would poison the queue.
Use `pytest-asyncio` (auto mode is on). Tests touching the DB should use a real test DB or transactional fixtures, not mocks of the session. Flag overuse of `MagicMock` for SQLAlchemy or httpx.
Secrets (GitHub App private key, DB URL, API keys) come from `gita.config` settings — never hardcoded, never read from `os.environ` directly outside `config.py`.

files.description: Authoritative dependency + tooling config. Ruff selects E/F/I/UP/B/SIM with line-length 100; don't comment on style issues already covered.

other.content: Project layout: `agents/` = PR reviewer + onboarding agents, `cli/` = `gita` entrypoint, `db/` = async SQLAlchemy models + migrations, `github/` = App auth + API client, `indexer/` = tree-sitter parsing + incremental git-diff indexing, `jobs/` + `worker.py` = arq background jobs, `llm/` = Anthropic client wrapper, `views/` = query/response schemas, `web/` = FastAPI app. Roadmap: Week 6+ adds branch creation and test generation — review comments that would block that direction (e.g., agents that can't write files) should be flagged.
